 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-a5z","title":"Add suno2cd to content.ts (11 stars)","description":"## What
Add suno2cd project to content.ts

## Project Details
- **Name**: suno2cd
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/suno2cd
- **Stars**: 11
- **Description**: "Convert Suno AI audio to CD quality (44.1kHz 16-bit WAV) in your browser"

## Background
- Suno is an AI music generation platform
- Suno outputs may not be at CD-quality sample rate/bit depth
- This tool upsamples/converts to standard CD format (44.1kHz 16-bit)
- Runs entirely in browser - no server needed
- Research at /data/projects/suno2cd/

## Implementation
```typescript
{
  name: "Suno2CD",
  kind: "oss",
  badge: "11 stars",
  href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/suno2cd",
  short: "Convert Suno AI audio to CD quality in your browser.",
  description: "Browser-based tool that converts Suno AI-generated music to CD-quality format (44.1kHz 16-bit WAV). No server required—all processing happens locally for privacy and speed.",
  tags: ["Audio", "Suno", "Web", "Conversion", "Music"],
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project added with description of the Suno-to-CD conversion use case","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:26:48.373977287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:21:09.601616379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:21:09.601616379Z","close_reason":"Added projects to content.ts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-a63l","title":"Add React.memo to tldr-tool-grid subcomponents","description":"# React.memo for TldrToolGrid Subcomponents

## Part of Feature
React Rendering Optimization

## Problem Statement
The `TldrToolGrid` component has three subcomponents that re-render unnecessarily when the parent's search state changes:

1. **ToolSearchBar** - Re-renders when grid results update
2. **EmptySearchState** - Re-renders on every keystroke
3. **SectionHeader** - Re-renders when search filters change

### The Re-render Cascade
```
User types in search
  → searchQuery state changes
    → TldrToolGrid re-renders
      → ToolSearchBar re-renders (unnecessary if props stable)
      → SectionHeader re-renders (unnecessary)
      → EmptySearchState re-renders (unnecessary)
      → All tool cards re-render via AnimatePresence
```

## Solution Implemented

### Added memo Import
```tsx
import { useState, useMemo, useRef, useEffect, memo } from "react";
```

### Wrapped Each Subcomponent

#### ToolSearchBar
```tsx
const ToolSearchBar = memo(function ToolSearchBar({
  query,
  onQueryChange,
  resultCount,
  totalCount,
  inputRef,
  reducedMotion,
}: { ... }) {
  return (
    // ... component body unchanged
  );
});
```

#### EmptySearchState
```tsx
const EmptySearchState = memo(function EmptySearchState({
  query,
  onClear,
  reducedMotion,
}: { ... }) {
  return (
    // ... component body unchanged
  );
});
```

#### SectionHeader
```tsx
const SectionHeader = memo(function SectionHeader({
  title,
  description,
  icon: Icon,
  count,
  reducedMotion,
}: { ... }) {
  return (
    // ... component body unchanged
  );
});
```

## Technical Details

### Why Named Function Expressions
```tsx
const Component = memo(function Component({ ... }) { ... });
```

Instead of:
```tsx
const Component = memo(({ ... }) => { ... });
```

**Reasons:**
1. Better React DevTools display (shows "Component" not "Anonymous")
2. Better stack traces in errors
3. Better debugging experience
4. Recommended React pattern

### Props Stability Analysis

**ToolSearchBar Props:**
- `query`: string (changes on input)
- `onQueryChange`: callback (stable via parent)
- `resultCount`/`totalCount`: numbers (changes on filter)
- `inputRef`: ref (stable)
- `reducedMotion`: boolean (stable after mount)

React.memo performs shallow comparison. When props don't change, render is skipped.

**SectionHeader Props:**
- `title`, `description`: strings (static)
- `icon`: component (stable reference)
- `count`: number (changes on filter)
- `reducedMotion`: boolean (stable)

Static props remain stable, only `count` changes when results filter.

## Isomorphic Guarantee
✅ **Same output**: memo wraps component, doesn't change its output
✅ **Same behavior**: Component renders identically when it does render
✅ **Shallow comparison**: Props compared by reference (standard React behavior)

## Files Modified
- `components/tldr-tool-grid.tsx`
  - Line 3: Added `memo` to imports
  - Lines 24-116: Wrapped ToolSearchBar with memo
  - Lines 122-157: Wrapped EmptySearchState with memo
  - Lines 163-202: Wrapped SectionHeader with memo

## Verification
```bash
npm run lint  # 0 errors
npm run build # Success
```

## Performance Impact
- **Search typing**: Subcomponents skip re-render when their props unchanged
- **Initial render**: No impact (first render always runs)
- **Typical improvement**: 3 fewer component renders per search keystroke

## Why Not Memoize TldrToolCard?
The `TldrToolCard` component is already expensive to render (many DOM nodes, animations), but:
1. It receives `tool` object prop that changes on filter
2. Cards are already filtered in/out via AnimatePresence
3. Adding memo to each card would add overhead without benefit

The cards that render are the ones that SHOULD render.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:51:14.621242694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:53.898658302Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:53:53.898658302Z","close_reason":"Wrapped ToolSearchBar, EmptySearchState, SectionHeader with React.memo in tldr-tool-grid.tsx. Prevents unnecessary re-renders when parent state changes but props are stable. Commit: 1c41ca0","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}

2. Tools configured (30+ tools)?

### Implementation

2. Idempotent execution?
3. Error handling?
4. Platform support?

### Integrations
1. How does it set up the full flywheel?
2. Post-install verification?

## Deliverable

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-azd","title":"Set up GitHub Action for automated stargazer updates","description":"# Task: Set Up GitHub Action for Automated Stargazer Updates

## Context

Automate the stargazer analysis script to run weekly via GitHub Actions. This ensures the data stays fresh without manual intervention, and commits results directly to the repo for use at build time.

## Why P3?

This is enhancement work—the feature works without automation (manual script runs). However, automation is important for:
- Keeping data fresh long-term
- Reducing maintenance burden
- Ensuring consistency

## GitHub Token Clarification

**Good news: The default GITHUB_TOKEN should work for your own repos\!**

The default `GITHUB_TOKEN` provided by GitHub Actions has `contents: read` permission for the current repo AND can read public repos. Since we're analyzing Jeffrey's own public repos, this should work without creating a separate PAT.

Try the default token first:
```yaml
env:
  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

Only create a PAT if you hit permission issues (unlikely for public repo analysis).

## Deliverables

### 1. GitHub Actions Workflow

File: `.github/workflows/update-stargazers.yml`

```yaml
name: Update Stargazer Intelligence

on:
  schedule:
    # Weekly on Sunday at 3 AM UTC
    - cron: '0 3 * * 0'
  workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger

permissions:
  contents: write  # Needed for committing changes

jobs:
  analyze:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Bun
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
        
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install
        
      - name: Run stargazer analysis
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # Default token should work\!
        run: bun run scripts/analyze-stargazers.ts
        
      - name: Commit and push if changed
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config --local user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git add lib/data/stargazer-intelligence.json
          git diff --staged --quiet || git commit -m "chore: update stargazer intelligence data"
          git push
```

### 2. If Default Token Doesn't Work (Fallback)

Only if you hit issues:
1. Create PAT at github.com/settings/tokens with `public_repo` scope
2. Add as repo secret: `STARGAZER_ANALYSIS_TOKEN`
3. Update workflow to use it

## Schedule Considerations

**Weekly (recommended)**
- Star data changes slowly
- Reduces API usage
- Sufficient for credibility display

## Testing

1. Manually trigger workflow via GitHub UI (`workflow_dispatch`)
2. Verify script runs successfully
3. Check commit appears in repo
4. Verify JSON file is valid
5. Trigger build to confirm data is used

## Dependencies

- Depends on: Script task (must exist and work)
- No downstream blockers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Workflow file created and valid
- [ ] Manual trigger works
- [ ] Scheduled run executes successfully
- [ ] Changes committed automatically
- [ ] Default GITHUB_TOKEN confirmed working (or PAT set up if needed)

## Future Enhancements (out of scope)

- Conditional runs (skip if no new stars)
- PR-based updates instead of direct commits
- Failure notifications beyond GitHub defaults","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:53:09.145805616Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T06:00:25.835659532Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T06:00:25.835659532Z","close_reason":"Closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-azd","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-8mq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:54:43.853520879Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
